title: Tectaria zeilanica
taxonomy: Tectaria zeilanica belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes over 14,000 species of ferns worldwide. The genus Tectaria is a part of this family and includes several species of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Tectaria zeilanica is one of the species within the genus and is classified based on its unique characteristics and morphological features. The classification of Tectaria zeilanica is subject to ongoing research and revisions in the field of botany.
uses: Tectaria zeilanica is utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are often used in herbal remedies to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In some cultures, it is also valued for its antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote the recovery process.
